routes.description:
Serve static files from frontend/dist; fallback to index.html for SPA routes.

If the build output is missing, return a friendly message suggesting to
run `npm run build` in the frontend directory.

Serve static files from frontend/dist; fallback to index.html for SPA routes.

If the build output is missing, return a friendly message suggesting to
run `npm run build` in the frontend directory.
Append an "aborted" entry for the given agent_id to the log service.
Superadmin creates a new organization.
List organizations. Superadmin sees all; admin and user see their own org.
Superadmin deletes an organization. Blocked if org has any users.
Superadmin updates any org. Admin updates their own org. Fields: org_name, description.
Return a summary of active/idle users on AMP for admin roles.
LLM-powered assistant for presence dashboard questions.
Provision a new user (email + org) without a password.
List users. Superadmin sees all; admin sees users in their own org.
Superadmin edits any user's profile and role. Admin edits users in their company.

Mark a task as completed (for remote agents).

Remote agents call this after successfully processing a task.

Body:
    agent_name: Name of the agent (required)
    task_id: ID of the completed task (required)

Returns:
    JSON with 'ok' field

Poll for pending queued tasks (for remote agents).

This endpoint exists for legacy/optional queued-task workflows.
Launch/abort in AMP are direct calls and do not enqueue tasks.
Agents also auto-register themselves on first poll.

Query params:
    agent_name: Name of the agent (required)
    owner: Owner's email/username (required)
    long_name: Display name (optional, for registration)
    description: Agent description (optional, for registration)

Returns:
    JSON with 'tasks' array

Deprecated: register a remote agent. Agent ID is auto-generated from display name.

Body:
    long_name: Display name for the agent (required)
    description: Agent description (optional)
    auto_start: Allow agent-initiated instances to start immediately (optional, default false)

Returns:
    JSON with registered agent info including generated agent_name

Unregister a remote agent (remove from registry).

This allows users to remove stale or unwanted remote agents.
Requires authentication - users can only unregister their own agents.

Body:
    agent_name: Name of the agent to unregister (required)

Returns:
    JSON with 'ok' field
Get autonomy_ops from policy.json for an agent.
Save autonomy_ops to policy.json for an agent.
Get criteria from policy.json for an agent.
Save criteria to policy.json for an agent.
Save and approve an eval-type policy (save criteria + version + approve in one shot).
Deactivate the current active eval policy. The agent will have no active policy afterward.
Reactivate the latest (highest-numbered) eval policy version.
Get policy.json for an agent (features section).
Save policy.json for an agent (features section).
Generate a description for a feature field using LLM.
Get a specific policy version.
Update RLHF enabled status for the current policy.
Get all policy versions for an agent.
Approve and version the RLHF policy for an agent.
Create a new instance via API key auth (agent-initiated).

Set lifecycle status in instance meta.json.

Payload:
    agent_name: str (required)
    instance_id: str (required)
    state: one of initiated|active|finished|wait|abort (required)
Deprecated alias for /api/agent/init.
Return parsed records for the requested month/year; defaults to current.
Return the closest LLM trace entry for an instance and timestamp.

List available ALP agents from AGENT_ROOT.

An agent is any directory under AGENT_ROOT that contains a config.json.
Returns objects with { name, long_name, description }.
Return agent-level default config and prompt.
List artifact files stored at the agent root (template artifacts).
Serve agent-level (template) artifacts.
Persist agent-level default config.json.
Persist agent-level default prompt.txt.
List ALP instances for a given agent by reading its subfolders with meta.json.

Delete an ALP instance directory recursively.

Returns 200 with { deleted: true } on success. If the instance directory
is missing, returns 200 with { deleted: false, missing: true } for idempotency.
Return meta, config, and prompt for a specific instance. Falls back to agent defaults when missing.

Send abort signal to agent instance and log the event.

Direct mode only:
- Call agent API immediately.

List artifact files under an instance's artifacts directory.

Returns a list of { name, size, mtime, ext, content_type }.

Upload an artifact file from remote agent.

Body: raw file data
Remote agents use this to upload output files (results, reports, etc.)
Serve an artifact file inline when possible (browser decides how to open).
Chat with the LLM configured for this agent instance.
Update instance config.json; allowed only before status becomes 'active'.
Proxy endpoint to execute agent actions securely.

Get all instance files for remote agent.

Returns config.json, prompt.txt, and meta.json.
This endpoint is used by remote agents to download their instance files.
Deprecated alias for /api/hitl/agents/<name>/instances/<instance_id>/callback.

Transition an ALP instance from 'initiated' to 'active'.

Direct mode only:
- Call agent API immediately.

Append a 'state - initiated' entry for a specific ALP instance.

- Validates the instance directory exists for the agent.
- Updates the instance's meta.json status to 'initiated' and sets owner to current username.
- Appends a state change log line to the current month AMP logfile.

Update meta.json for remote agent.

Body should be a partial or full meta.json object.
Remote agents use this to update status, timestamps, etc.
Update instance prompt.txt; allowed only before status becomes 'active'.
Return the contents of summary.txt for an instance, if present.
Write summary.txt for an instance.
Create a new instance folder with provided config/prompt; status 'initiated'.

Register a native agent: adds entry to org's config.json and creates agent directory.

Body:
    long_name: Display name for the agent (required)
    name: Agent slug/folder name (required, lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens)
    description: Agent description (optional)
    status: Agent status (optional, default "active")

Returns:
    JSON with registered agent info, or 409 if name already taken
Generate a conversational response about AMP activity using authorized records.
Persist and retrieve AMP chat transcripts per user.
SSE endpoint for frontend connections to receive real-time refresh signals.
Debug endpoint to check current SSE connections.
Trigger endpoint for logger service to call when new records are written.

Return org-scoped agent_events rows as a JSON array.

Query params: start_date, end_date (YYYY-MM-DD; default last 30 days).
superadmin may pass ?org_id= to override scope.
Return the current analytics data source configuration.

Return org-scoped hitl_events rows as a JSON array.

Query params: start_date, end_date (YYYY-MM-DD; default last 30 days).
superadmin may pass ?org_id= to override scope.
Record a page view counter for the current user.

Return org-scoped RLHF approval_events aggregated by date and agent.

Query params: start_date, end_date (YYYY-MM-DD; default last 90 days).
superadmin may pass ?org_id= to override scope.

Return org-scoped current autonomy stage and stage transition history.

Returns: { current: [{agent_name, autonomy_stage, stage_entered_at,
                       model_version, kill_switch_enabled}],
           transitions: [{agent_name, from_stage, to_stage, actor_type,
                           reason, timestamp, previous_model_version,
                           new_model_version}] }
superadmin may pass ?org_id= to override scope.

Return org-scoped user_activity_daily rows as a JSON array.

Query params: start_date, end_date (YYYY-MM-DD; default last 30 days).
superadmin may pass ?org_id= to override scope.
Proxy to llm_client.classify_intent for chat command understanding.
Expose frontend config including available agent names and LLM settings.
LLM chat endpoint for the AMP dashboard.
Serve dashboard demo CSVs from the backend.

Submit a HITL request and optionally register a completion callback.

This endpoint forwards the request payload to the internal HITL service,
which evaluates WHEN/WHO/WHAT/WHERE and either:
- returns immediately with `status=no-hitl`, or
- accepts the request and waits for human/auto completion.

Request JSON (fields commonly used by callers):
{
  "caller_id": "<instance-or-activity-id>",
  "instance_id": "<alias of caller_id>",
  "org_id": "<organization id>",
  "agent_name": "<agent name>",
  "rlhf": {
    "policy_values_source": "external|internal",
    "action_context": { "...": "..." },
    "facts_context": { "...": "..." },
    "criteria": [ { "...": "..." } ],
    "evidence": { "...": "..." },
    "run_mode": "<optional>",
    "sim_run_id": "<optional>",
    "sim_time": "<optional>",
    "checkpoint_id": "<optional>"
  },
  "hitl": {
    "enable": true,
    "when": "<condition|always>",
    "who": "<initiator|email>",
    "what": "approval|notify",
    "where": "amp|email"
  },
  "callback": {
    "url": "https://caller.example.com/hitl/callback",
    "secret": "<optional shared secret>",
    "timeout_ms": 5000
  }
}

Callback payload on completion (single attempt, best effort):
{
  "caller_id": "<id>",
  "status": "complete",
  "resolution": "approve|reject|modify|defer|unknown",
  "method": "human|auto",
  "workitem_id": "<id_or_empty>",
  "information": "<optional detail>",
  "event_ts": "<ISO-8601 UTC timestamp>"
}
Proxy HITL progress requests.
Proxy HITL status requests.
Canonical HITL callback route for agent instances.
Return normalized HITL decision state for a caller without exposing stage routing details.
Proxy HITL progress requests.

Submit a HITL request and optionally register a completion callback.

This endpoint forwards the request payload to the internal HITL service,
which evaluates WHEN/WHO/WHAT/WHERE and either:
- returns immediately with `status=no-hitl`, or
- accepts the request and waits for human/auto completion.

Request JSON (fields commonly used by callers):
{
  "caller_id": "<instance-or-activity-id>",
  "instance_id": "<alias of caller_id>",
  "org_id": "<organization id>",
  "agent_name": "<agent name>",
  "rlhf": {
    "policy_values_source": "external|internal",
    "action_context": { "...": "..." },
    "facts_context": { "...": "..." },
    "criteria": [ { "...": "..." } ],
    "evidence": { "...": "..." },
    "run_mode": "<optional>",
    "sim_run_id": "<optional>",
    "sim_time": "<optional>",
    "checkpoint_id": "<optional>"
  },
  "hitl": {
    "enable": true,
    "when": "<condition|always>",
    "who": "<initiator|email>",
    "what": "approval|notify",
    "where": "amp|email"

    "secret": "<optional shared secret>",
    "timeout_ms": 5000
  }
}

Callback payload on completion (single attempt, best effort):
{

  "status": "complete",

  "method": "human|auto",
  "workitem_id": "<id_or_empty>",
  "information": "<optional detail>",
  "event_ts": "<ISO-8601 UTC timestamp>"
}
Proxy HITL status requests.
Internal helper to resolve org_id/user_id for a username.
Classify a prompt using the configured LLM (internal-only).

Proxy endpoint for logging requests from remote agents.
Forwards requests to internal Logger service.

Proxy a HITL progress request to the log service.

Forwards query parameters and any internal secret header. Returns 503
if the log service is unreachable.

Proxy a progress-all status request to the log service.

Forwards query parameters and any internal secret header. Returns 503
if the log service is unreachable.
Append a USER/LLM exchange to logs/amp.log for later auditing/debugging.
Authenticate a user and issue an in-memory session token.
Invalidate the caller's active session.
Allow a user to change their password by providing the current password.
Set an initial password for a user record that lacks a stored hash.
List all template folders in the policy library (read-only).
Read a policy.json template from the policy library (read-only).
Record a heartbeat indicating the caller is active.
Create a new local user in the database using PBKDF2-SHA256.
Fetch a stored RLHF approval request by decision_point_id.
Bootstrap rlhf_agent_config/state from policy and optionally enqueue STAGE_TICK.

Approve an RLHF policy contract binding the policy to an org and agent.

Calculates an approval hash over the policy, allowlists, and approver info,
then stores the contract record in the database.

Atomically bind an RLHF decision request to a workitem for human-routed decisions.
Returns deterministic routing fields for client integrators.
Deterministic status for one decision_point_id (routing, mode, and workitem linkage).
Admin endpoint for deterministic RLHF job enqueue (E2E + ops).
List model versions in the registry, optionally filtered by org/agent/status.

Activate a model version. Retires the currently active model for
the same (org_id, agent_name) and marks this one as active.
Superadmin only.
Retire a model version. Superadmin only.
Submit RLHF approval outcome to HITL agent (internal wiring).

Retrieve the active RLHF policy for an agent.

Returns the policy JSON, version, feature schema version, current autonomy
stage, and RLHF status flags.

Save a new RLHF policy JSON for an agent.

Validates against the policy schema, stamps the version, and persists via
_save_rlhf_policy. Returns the saved version and policy.
Enqueue deterministic STAGE_TICK job for accelerated/local test runs.
Admin runtime status for one (org_id, agent_name) RLHF pipeline.
Reset RLHF runtime tables for one org+agent to support repeatable E2E runs.
Create a new API key with an optional label.
List all active API keys for the current user.
Revoke a specific API key by its key_id.
Update the label on a specific API key.

Decrypt and return the raw key for a specific key_id.

Returns 404 if the key has no stored ciphertext (created before encryption
was added, or encryption was unavailable at creation time).
Return the authenticated user's own profile.
Update the authenticated user's own profile fields.
Create a workitem; remove prior completed items with the same instance_id and username.
Get workitems for a specific user.
Delete a specific workitem.
Update the status of a specific workitem.
Chat endpoint for worktray page with access to workitems context.
